headline: CATTLE FADE AS GRAINS MARK TIME
subheadline: Live cattle slip from breakout levels while corn and beans hold tight ranges.
lead: Cattle dropped 0.4% to $254.03, the first real test since breaking above $250 on momentum three days ago. The pullback is mild but the funds are watching — managed money added 8,000 contracts this week and needs to see the floor hold. Grains stayed pinned in place with corn at $4.78 and beans flat at $12.03, waiting for next week's planting progress to matter.
teaser: Cattle test breakout support while grains wait for planting data to drive direction.

one_number.context: First pullback since the breakout above $250 three sessions ago. The level is holding but fund money that drove the rally is testing patience. Below $252 breaks the pattern.

yesterdays_call.summary: Price remaining summer cattle inventory above $250 as the breakout had legs.
yesterdays_call.note: Cattle closed at $254.03, holding the breakout level but showing first real weakness since the rally began.

sections.title: Cattle Test Breakout
sections.body: Live cattle dropped 0.4% to $254.03, the first meaningful pullback since breaking above $250 three sessions ago. The decline tests fund conviction — managed money added 8,000 contracts this week and needs to see support hold. Cash trade stayed firm in the mid-$190s, keeping the premium structure intact. The chart wants to see $252 hold as the new floor.
sections.bottom_line: Breakout getting its first real test — $252 is the line.
sections.farmer_action: Hold pricing discipline above $252 — below that level questions the breakout thesis.

sections.title: Grains Mark Time
sections.body: Corn eased a quarter cent to $4.78 while soybeans stayed flat at $12.03, both trapped in tight ranges waiting for catalysts. December corn at $4.97 shows the carry structure is working but lacks conviction. The funds are net long but not adding positions — open interest dropped for the third straight session. Next week's planting progress report could break the deadlock if weather turns unfavorable.
sections.bottom_line: Coiled ranges plus planting data next week equals potential resolution.

spread_to_watch.label: Live cattle / feeder ratio
spread_to_watch.level: 68.0 ratio, compressing from 69.2 two weeks ago
spread_to_watch.commentary: Feeder cattle gained 0.3% while live cattle dropped, compressing the ratio to the tightest level in three weeks. The narrowing spread says backgrounding margins are improving as feed costs ease and placement demand stays steady.

basis.headline: Corn basis steady as ethanol margins hold firm
basis.body: Eastern Corn Belt basis staying flat to 5 over as ethanol grind maintains pace after maintenance season. Western basis eased a nickel but remains within seasonal norms. Soybean basis firmed 2-3 cents as crush plants compete for remaining old-crop supplies ahead of new-crop availability.

weekly_thread.question: Can cattle hold above $250 or was this week's breakout just a head fake?
weekly_thread.status_text: The test came Friday with cattle dropping to $254.03, the first real pullback since breaking $250. The level held but fund conviction is now being measured in real time.

the_more_you_know.title: Why Quiet Days Matter More Than Volatile Ones
the_more_you_know.body: Today's flat grain action with corn at $4.78 and beans at $12.03 tells you more than big moves. When volatility drops and ranges compress, it signals the market is waiting for new information rather than processing old news. The funds aren't adding positions — corn open interest dropped for three straight sessions — which means conviction is low. These quiet periods often precede significant moves when catalysts arrive, like next week's planting progress data.

watch_list.desc: USDA Crop Progress; corn planting below 35% nationally adds weather premium
Weekly cattle cash trade; mid-$190s maintains premium structure
Weekly export sales; soybeans under 200K MT keeps chart in charge

daily_quote.text: Plans are nothing; planning is everything.

source_summary: CME futures, USDA reports, cash market data
